data.title: Advantage Energy gives FY results
data.description:
• Advantage Energy press release (AAV:CA): FY GAAP EPS of $0.31.
  • Revenue of $698.98M (+28.7% Y/Y).
  • Adjusted funds flow of $381.6 million or $2.29/share for Advantage.
  • Cash used in investing activities of $422.0 million, including both Advantage and Entropy.

data.title: Methode Electronics Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.37, revenue of $233.7M
data.description:
• Methode Electronics press release (MEI): Q3 Non-GAAP EPS of -$0.37.
  • Revenue of $233.7M (-2.6% Y/Y)
  • The Company revised its fiscal 2026 full year guidance and now expects full-year fiscal 2026 net sales to be in the range of $950 million to $1 billion, narrowing its prior outlook of $900 million to $1 billion.
  • The Company lowered its fiscal 2026 full year Adjusted EBITDA guidance and now expects Adjusted EBITDA to be in the range of $58 to $62 million.

data.title: Ingram Micro shareholder prices 8.99M-share offering at $22.25
data.description:
• Ingram Micro (INGM) announced the pricing of a secondary public offering of around 8.99M shares at $22.25 per share.
  • The shares are being sold by Ingram Holdco, LLC, an affiliate of Platinum Equity.
  • The selling stockholder also gave underwriters a 30-day option to buy up to 1.35M additional shares. All proceeds from the offering will go to the selling stockholder, and Ingram Micro will not receive any proceeds.
  • Separately, the company plans to repurchase $75M of its shares from the selling stockholder using cash on hand, with the transaction expected to close around March 9, 2026.
  • INGM shares fell -13.2% to $22.88 after-hours on Thursday.

data.title: China approves Pfizer’s GLP-1 drug for weight management, intensifying market competition
data.description:
• China has approved a new obesity drug from Pfizer (PFE), intensifying competition in a market that is expected to grow even more crowded with upcoming generic versions.
  • The drug, ecnoglutide, is approved for chronic weight management in overweight or obese adults, Pfizer (PFE) announced on Friday via WeChat, according to reports. The company secured China rights to the therapy from local startup Hangzhou Sciwind Bioscience in a $495 million deal in late February. Ecnoglutide ​is also ​approved in China as a treatment for Type II diabetes.
  • The injection belongs to the class of GLP-1 receptor agonist drugs sold locally by drugmakers such as Novo Nordisk (NVO), ‌Eli Lilly (LLY), and ⁠Innovent Biologics (IVBIY).

data.title: AES Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.20, revenue of $3.1B beats by $30M
data.description:
• AES press release (AES): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.81 beats by $0.20.
  • Revenue of $3.1B (+4.7% Y/Y) beats by $30M.

data.title: Rentokil Initial plc GAAP EPS of $0.11, revenue of $6.91B
data.description:
• Rentokil Initial plc press release (RTO): FY GAAP EPS of $0.11.
  • Revenue of $6.91B (+4.4% Y/Y).
  • FY25 Group Organic Revenue Growth of 2.6%, with improved performance in the second half.
  • Free Cash Flow1 of $615m, up 24.5% year-on-year, and Free Cash Flow Conversion of 98%, ahead of guidance due to real-estate sales and some one-off benefits within the overall working capital improvement.
  • Recommended final dividend of 8.24 cents, up 4.6%; total FY25 dividend of 12.39 cents, up 3%.
  • 2026 Outlook
     
    ●
     
    Despite some weather-related disruption in North America in January, and increased uncertainty from recent geopolitical events, we expect to deliver FY 2026 financial results in line with market expectations.

data.title: U.S. issues 30‑day waiver allowing India to buy Russian oil as Iran war disrupts supplies
data.description:
The United States on Thursday issued a 30-day waiver to India for purchasing crude from Moscow as the Iran war upends global supplies. The waiver on purchasing Russian oil is expected to help ease global supply worries, with India being the world’s fourth-biggest refiner and fifth-largest exporter of petroleum products. 

A license issued late on Thursday covers transactions related to Russian crude oil and petroleum products loaded onto vessels before March 5, so long as they're delivered to India and purchased by an Indian firm. The measure expires April 4 at 12:01 a.m. Washington time.

India faces risks from energy supply disruptions, with crude oil reserves sufficient for only about 25 days of demand. Around 40% of the country’s oil imports pass through the Strait of Hormuz from the Middle East.

Brent (CO1:COM) and WTI crude (CL1:COM) recouped some losses after falling over 1% on Friday and were last trading at $85.19 and $80.60 per barrel, respectively.

This “short-term measure will not provide significant financial benefit” to Russia as it only allows transactions of oil already stranded at sea, U.S. Secretary of the Treasury Scott Bessant said in a post on X.

The U.S. government, meanwhile, is taking steps to curb rising oil prices, including offering political risk insurance for tankers transiting the Gulf. U.S. crude prices have climbed about 20% this week on the back of the escalating conflict in the Middle East.

data.title: REalloys prices 2.7M share public offering at $18.50 each, stock dips
data.description:
• REalloys (ALOY) on Friday said that it has priced its previously announced underwritten public offering of 2,702,702 common shares at $18.50 per share.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about March 9, 2026.
  • The company's shares (ALOY) were down 10% in extended trading to $19.45; it has gained over 165% YTD.

data.title: Hunting PLC Non-GAAP EPS of $0.34, revenue of $1.02B misses by $70M
data.description:
• Hunting PLC press release (HNTIF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of $0.34.
  • Revenue of $1.02B (-2.9% Y/Y) misses by $70M.
  • Non-oil and gas revenue up 10%, to $82.9m.
  • Sales order book $358.0m comprising $120.7m of subsea and $98.6m of non-oil and gas opportunities.
  • Free cash flow of $96.6m – representing an EBITDA conversion of 71%.
  • Cash and bank / (borrowings) $62.9m, after c.$145m of net outflows related to acquisitions, share buybacks, treasury share purchases, and dividends.
  • 2026 EBITDA guidance of $145-$155m retained, with EBITDA to Free Cash Flow conversion targeted at 50% or greater.

data.title: Profound Medical outlines path to $85M revenue and profitable growth with TULSA expansion in 2026
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Profound Medical Corp. (PRN:CA) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Arun Menawat, Chairman & CEO, emphasized the completion of recruitment for CAPTAIN, the first multicenter, randomized, controlled trial comparing a new technology to robotic radical prostatectomy, establishing "the foundational pillars of clinical evidence, validating TULSA as the new platform for prostate disease management." Menawat highlighted the imminent release of CAPTAIN’s primary safety and quality of life endpoints as a catalyst, stating, "CAPTAIN provides the first true apples-to-apples comparison of safety, quality of life and efficacy, the information required to support a new treatment paradigm."
  • Mathieu Burtnyk, President, announced that clinical outcomes from CAPTAIN will be presented ahead of schedule at the European Association of Urology meeting, including "complete 90-day perioperative results and the 6-month primary safety and quality of life endpoints." He noted, "Six-month quality of life outcomes are an increasingly important and modern endpoint."
  • Thomas Tamberrino, Chief Commercial Officer, reported that as of year-end, there were "78 TULSA-PRO sites" and a "TULSA-PRO qualified sales pipeline...at 110 new systems." He projected that, with current strategies, "we would be at $55 million in procedural revenue" and "around $85 million in annual revenue" when including service and capital sales, with profitability achieved at this scale.
  • Rashed Dewan, CFO, stated, "For the 3 months period ended, December 31, 2025, the company recorded revenue of $6 million, with $2.3 million from recurring revenue and $3.7 million from onetime sales of capital equipment."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated expectations to reach "high double-digit to low triple-digit revenue growth" in 2026. Tamberrino described, "Importantly, we believe we are now on a path to not just growth but profitable growth with this selling approach." Menawat stated, "We expect our cash burn to decline and eventually turn cash flow positive as our revenues continue to grow and our margin remains high."
  • No explicit numerical revenue or EPS guidance was provided for 2026, but the company reaffirmed its focus on expanding the installed base and driving utilization, aiming for "approximately 120" TULSA-PRO sites by year-end 2026.

Financial Results

  • Profound Medical recorded Q4 2025 revenue of $6 million, with recurring revenue at $2.3 million and capital equipment sales at $3.7 million. This represented a 43% increase from $4.2 million in Q4 2024.
  • Gross margin for Q4 2025 was 67%, down from 71% in Q4 2024, attributed to product mix and introductory pricing for new international distributors.
  • Total operating expenses for Q4 2025 were $11.4 million, compared to $11.3 million in the prior-year quarter.
  • The company reported a net loss of $8.2 million, or ($0.27) per share, versus a net loss of $4.9 million, or ($0.20) per share, in Q4 2024.
  • Cash as of December 31, 2025, stood at $59.7 million.

Q&A

  • Benjamin Haynor, Lake Street Capital Markets: Asked about commercial insurer reimbursement rates and success in overturning denials. Menawat responded, "The typical payments are between -- I would say, most of them are between 1.5x to 2.5x of Medicare...coverage and reversals from rejections were tracking better than 90% at this point."
  • Haynor: Inquired about the sequential decline in noncapital revenue and utilization. Menawat explained, "Every site is slowly but surely increasing usage...the pipeline is pretty strong. But over the long haul, I think that, we remain primarily a recurring revenue company."
  • John McAulay, Stifel: Sought clarity on recurring revenue per procedure and future revenue mix. Menawat clarified, "Our price for disposables is $5,500 fixed. There is absolutely no discounting on the disposable price."
  • Michael Freeman, Raymond James: Asked about early release of CAPTAIN data and impact on reimbursement. Menawat commented, "We are very careful...our trials are pristine...I do expect that the earlier data will suddenly gives us more confidence in getting coverage decisions this year."
  • Scott McAuley, Paradigm Capital: Asked about BPH module adoption and international market progress. Menawat stated, "At least 10 sites...have already started using it. By midyear, we will have at least 30, 40 sites using it."
  • Christopher Potter, Northern Border Advisors: Queried about procedure utilization per site. Menawat responded, "We think 50 is a very reasonable number...We have sites today that are doing well over 100."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on reimbursement, revenue mix, and site utilization, with a tone that was inquisitive to slightly skeptical, evidenced by detailed queries on recurring revenue fluctuations, per-procedure economics, and near-term utilization growth.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout, especially in prepared remarks. During Q&A, management remained composed and provided detailed clarifications, using phrases such as "we are very, very satisfied with the numbers that we're seeing" and "we are comfortable and confident" regarding growth and cash flow prospects.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's confidence level remained high, with a greater emphasis on strategic execution and operational improvements, while analyst tone remained focused on accountability for financial targets and utilization ramp.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue rose from $5.3 million in Q3 2025 to $6 million in Q4 2025, but gross margin declined from 74.3% to 67% due to introductory pricing for new international distributors.
  • The net loss widened to $8.2 million from $8 million, while cash increased significantly to $59.7 million from $24.8 million in the previous quarter.
  • Installed TULSA-PRO sites grew from 70 to 78, and the qualified sales pipeline increased from 93 to 110 new systems.
  • Management's tone shifted from emphasizing product launches and early BPH module feedback in Q3 to focusing on clinical evidence from CAPTAIN and international expansion in Q4.
  • Analysts continued to scrutinize revenue composition, utilization growth, and reimbursement, consistent with prior quarters, but with more granular questions on revenue per procedure and capital versus recurring revenue mix.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited challenges with gross margin pressure due to new market introductory pricing and product mix for international distributors.
  • There are operational scaling risks as the company navigates "increasing our logistics and operations side," including plans for a U.S. warehouse and expanded ERP systems.
  • The company faces the challenge of driving higher utilization per installed site to reach profitability targets.
  • Analyst questions reflected concerns about recurring revenue volatility, site ramp-up, and international expansion pacing.

Final Takeaway

Profound Medical management communicated a clear path toward profitable growth, underpinned by increasing TULSA-PRO installations, expanding international presence, and anticipated clinical milestones from the CAPTAIN trial. The company’s focus on high-margin recurring revenue, operational scaling, and reimbursement advances positions it to target $85 million in annual revenue and profitability, while ongoing clinical data releases and strategic partnerships are expected to drive further adoption and market penetration in 2026.
